mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate directly contracts consumer spending and increases credit defaults, which depresses corporate earnings and equity valuations over the subsequent month. Falsifiable if UNRATE rises but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances.
failure_condition: Rank IC of UNRATE against 20-day forward returns is non-negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1%, the central bank has entered an aggressive easing stance that compresses discount rates and signals policy support, leading to positive equity returns over the next quarter. Falsifiable if FEDFUNDS < 1% and SPX 60-day return is negative in more than 50% of episodes.
failure_condition: Average 60-day forward return after FEDFUNDS crosses below 1% is less than 2% or negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and retail sales reflect simultaneous weakening in both supply and demand channels, a powerful indicator of deepening recession that drives negative equity returns over 20 trading days. Falsifiable if both series decline but SPX rises in more than 40% of cases.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS are declining month-over-month, the median 20-day SPX return is positive or the hit rate of negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing bust regime where starts are below 1000 thousand units, further declines in HOUST signal persistent residential investment weakness and rising mortgage defaults, which drag on financial sector and overall equities over a 20-day horizon. Falsifiable if HOUST < 1000 and declines correlate with positive SPX.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000, the correlation between monthly HOUST changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is zero or positive.

mechanism_narrative: Falling real GDP signals a contracting aggregate economy that erodes corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. Falsifiable if GDP declines and SPX 60-day returns are positive in a majority of observations.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 (quarterly change) vs. 60-day forward returns is non-negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 136,000 thousand, it indicates severe labor market distress that depresses wage income and consumer confidence, translating into negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. Falsifiable if PAYEMS is below threshold and SPX 20-day return is positive in more than 40% of cases.
failure_condition: Average 20-day return after PAYEMS drops below 136000 is greater than 0.5% or positive in more than half of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero interest rates and collapsing retail sales indicates that monetary accommodation is failing to stimulate final demand, a toxic mix that predicts further equity downside over a 20-day horizon. Falsifiable if FEDFUNDS is low and RSAFS is falling but SPX rises in >35% of cases.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 1% and RSAFS declines month-over-month, the median SPX 20-day return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: During a deflation scare regime where headline CPI is contracting month-over-month, further declines in CPIAUCSL deepen expectations of deflation, increasing real debt burdens and depressing equity valuations over a quarter. Falsifiable if CPI falls but SPX 60-day return is positive in more than 40% of deflationary episodes.
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly CPIAUCSL declines and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is non-negative over any 2-year period containing deflation months.
